{"society_power": 6.0, "state_power": 1.5, "society_change": 0.0, "state_change": 0.0, "key_event": "Dervish revolt; no central state, strong clan society", "reasoning": "There was no unified Somali state\u2014territory was fragmented among competing European colonial claims (British, Italian) with minimal administrative reach and the Sayyid Mohammed Abdullah Hassan's Dervish movement openly defying them, so state power is near-collapsed. Meanwhile dense clan structures, customary law (xeer), and religious networks gave Somali society robust autonomous organizing and mobilization capacity, placing society power in the moderate range."}
